headline: Q1 2026 Earnings Roundup: Square Misses Revenue, Stripe Developers Surge, Adyen Volume Grows

competitors.name: Square
competitors.pricing_model: Square for Retail Plus missed Q1 revenue targets by 8 percent; company announced 49 dollar monthly price will hold through 2026 but hardware attach rate is declining
competitors.market_positioning: Physical retail focus under pressure from e-commerce shift; Cash App growth partially offsets hardware slowdown but investor confidence declining
competitors.confidence_notes: Block Q1 earnings call May 8 2026; CFO cited macro headwinds in SMB physical retail sector as primary drag

competitors.name: Stripe
competitors.pricing_model: Developer ecosystem growth accelerating despite pricing uncertainty; 2.9% plus 30 cents remains the active rate per latest available data at time of this report
competitors.market_positioning: Strong developer platform metrics with 40 percent year-over-year growth in new API integrations suggesting pricing reversion has stabilized the developer base
competitors.confidence_notes: Stripe private company metrics sourced from secondary market analysis and developer survey data May 2026

competitors.name: Adyen
competitors.pricing_model: Total processed volume up 22 percent year-over-year in Q1; interchange plus model generating strong net revenue retention among enterprise clients
competitors.market_positioning: Enterprise segment accelerating; new flat-rate tier expected to contribute meaningfully to 2026 transaction volume without cannibalizing enterprise margins
competitors.confidence_notes: Adyen Q1 2026 earnings published May 15; processed volume figures verified against Amsterdam stock exchange filing
